titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Norman Saunders Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 17, 2025 at 23:19

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains artwork, printed material, photographs, correspondence, personal papers, objects, and audiovisual and computer media by or relating to American illustrator Norman Saunders.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 46 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 47-48 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David Saunders on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2019, and gift of David Saunders, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Visual Material, 1886-2008. II. Correspondence, 1952-2005. III. Personal and Family Papers, 1927-2014. IV. Objects, circa 1945, undated. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1962-circa 2008.

bioghist.head: Norman Saunders (1907-1989)
bioghist.p: Norman Saunders was an American artist and illustrator of pulp magazines, paperbacks, men's adventure magazines, comic books, and trading cards. Born in Minot, North Dakota, and raised in Minnesota by Elva Cox and Clare Saunders, he began his career at Fawcett Publications and studied painting in New York under Harvey Dunn at the Grand Central School of Art. During World War II, Saunders served first in the Military Police, then in the Army Corps of Engineers, where he supervised the construction of a gas pipeline following the Burma Road; in his spare time, he photographed and sketched German prisoners of war and people, landscapes, and architecture in China and India. Saunders illustrated covers and content for magazines including Cap'n Billy's Whiz Bang , Modern Mechanics , Sports Afield , Saucy Movie Tales , Star Western , Sure Fire Detective , Romantic Range , Dime Detective , Max Brand's Western , Black Mask , Real Combat , World of Men , and Saga . He also contributed work for paperbacks published by Ace, Bantam, Ballantine, and Ellery Queen.

In 1958, Saunders began creating artwork for the trading cards company Topps Chewing Gum, Inc.; in addition to baseball cards, he contributed art for the 1962 "Mars Attacks" series; "Batman" TV series in 1966; and "Wacky Packages" series from 1967 to 1976. Saunders married model and editor Ellene Politis in 1947, and they had four children including Zina, an illustrator, and David, a painter and sculptor who published a book about his father, Norman Saunders , in 2008.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Saunders Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains artwork, printed material, photographs, correspondence, personal papers, objects, and audiovisual and computer media by or relating to American illustrator Norman Saunders.

Included are original pencil drawings and watercolors Saunders contributed to pulp magazines and paperback books, as well as tear sheets, cover proofs, puzzles, calendars, and other material featuring his illustrations. Other artwork by Saunders consists of drawings and paintings of German prisoners of war, members of his unit in the Army Corps of Engineers, and people and places in China and India during World War II; and scenes from his travels in Africa, Europe, and Greece.

The collection contains a large amount of reference photographs used by Saunders to create illustrations of women, detectives, cowboys, and Indigenous Americans; slides and prints of Saunders's artwork; photographs of the 1381st Engineer Petroleum Distribution Company, the Army unit overseeing construction of an oil pipeline along Burma Road during World War II; and family and personal portraits and snapshots.
Objects include a Polaroid camera, two prop Colt .45 guns depicted in Western-themed illustrations by Saunders, and an antique opium pipe and carved chopsticks he obtained in China.
The collection also contains papers of Saunders's son David, including a proof of his book Norman Saunders and associated research materials.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Norman Saunders Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: United States. Army. Corps of Engineers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains original drawings and paintings by Norman Saunders, and printed material and ephemera featuring his illustrations, such as tear sheets and covers from pulp magazines, paperbacks, and comic books, puzzles, and posters. Also included are proofs of trading cards, stickers, and display boxes created for Topps's "Wacky Packages," "Batman," and "Superman in the Jungle" series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Original Artwork, Illustrated Works, and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of pencil and watercolor drawings, as well as a few oil paintings by Saunders. Included are drawings of American soldiers, German prisoners of war, and scenes from the China-Burma-India Theater during World War II, abstract works, travel sketches, and illustrations for magazines and books. World War II materials formerly housed in binders were rehoused in folders in existing order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, with undated material filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drawings by Saunders created while he was in basic training at Fort Dix, Fort Ontario, Stoney Point.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #2 / June 1943 / Corporal in the military police

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes include "Jim Crow R.R. cars" and Nazi prisoners of war. Drawings often contain Saunders's handwritten observations on his subjects. Also includes extracts of Special Orders by the 768th Military Police Battalion, Fayetteville, New York, and Saunders's letters to his parents.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #3 /Engineers Petroleum Distribution Company 1381st / March 28, 1944 - Camp Claiborne, Louisiana / July 29, 1944 - to San Pedro, Cal / Sept. 1944 troop ship to Australia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Saunders and his brother Duane, and letters to his parents and Russell King.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #4 / October 1944 - Bombay India to Calcutta + up the Brahmaputra River to Ledo
WWII / Binder #5 / Nov. 5, 1944 Ledo Road - India - To the border of Ledo/Burma Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include letters to Russell King.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #6 / Nov. 18, 1944 India - Ledo Road - Flies the hump Dec. 22, 1944 / Jan. 16, 1945 China - Burma Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter to Duane Saunders with a photograph.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #7 / Yunnan, Shiquan, Mekong River / Dec. 1944 / Burma Road China / Jan. 1, 1945

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes several letters to Russell King and two "V-Mail" forms with illustrations.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #8 / Jan. 31, 1945 - China - Burma Road / Feb. 15, 1945 - General Pick's "Token Convoy"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Russell King

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #10 / China 1945 / Yunnan, Tali, Burma Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, letters to Russell King, correspondence from the Army about his pipeline sketches and attempts to have them published in Life magazine, photos of his parents, and a letter from Fawcett Publications.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #11 / 1945 China - Burma Road / Gasoline drums, temples, tobacco, opium, prostitutes
WWII / Binder #12 / China - 1945 - Burma Road / Tobacco, aluminum, temples, markets, air base Kunming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also depicts opium use, sex workers, and scenes from the Tinsukia district and town of Ledo in India.

did.unittitle: WWII / Binder #13 / Sept. 29, 1945 - Flies out of Kunming - Ledo - Acra - Karachi - NYC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters, photos of artwork, printed material.

Abstract painting, oil on board, signed on verso

Abstract painting, oil on board, signed on recto

did.unittitle: Watercolor and pencil drawings of summer vacation at Wings Farm in Ithaca, NY
Watercolor and pencil drawings of summer vacation at Fire Island, NY
Watercolor and pencil drawings of summer vacation at Fire Island, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red lines indicate use of dynamic symmetry.

did.unittitle: Watercolor drawings of street scene, Memorial Day event [?]
Watercolor drawings of summer vacation at Swiss Alps

Watercolor drawing of men next to fountain

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 31: In-process material pending conservation treatment/housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of cover proofs, magazine interiors, posters, trading cards, display boxes, stickers, one record album cover, and other materials illustrated by Saunders. Included are tear sheets from Modern Mechanics and Sports Afield , in addition to covers of pulp magazines such as Black Book Detective , Saucy Movie Tales , Mystery Adventure , Ten Detective Aces , and Western Trails ; comic books such as Hopalong Cassidy and Tom Mix ; and paperbacks from Ballantine Books, Bantam Books, and Pocket Detective. The subseries also contains Saunders's "Night Rider," "An Eye Full," and "A Handy Hat Rack" illustrations, reproduced on jigsaw puzzles and calendars for the A. Fox Litho company, Milton Bradley, and Fairchild Corp. Illustations created for Topps primarily include uncut sheets of trading cards and stickers for "Wacky Packages"; materials related to "Mars Attacks" are not present.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Magazines, Books, and Ephemera, and Topps Trading Card Collection. Within subheadings, materials are arranged in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs taken in China and India during Saunders's tour of duty in World War II; photographs, slides, and negatives of his artwork; reference photographs of Saunders and his wife Ellene in various poses and Hollywood movie stills used to model his illustrations; and a small amount of family and personal photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under five subheadings: World War II, Artwork by Saunders, Polaroids and Small Prints, Hollywood Reference, and Family and Personal.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Photographs are also located in Series II. Correspondence and Series III. Personal and Family Papers.

did.unittitle: Photos of the 1381st Engineer Petroleum Distribution Company / Burma Road WWII publications
Photograph album - 1381st / India and China [loose photos]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph taken by Gary Carty [?] in unidentified city possibly Montréal.

did.unittitle: "WWII Army G.I.s posed by Norm Saunders"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 8"x10" black and white photographs, mostly of Hollywood westerns and period movies of the early 1930s. Includes photographs of popular performers such as of Marlene Dietrich, Gary Cooper, Mary Pickford, Jimmy Stewart, Spencer Tracy, Warner Oland, Anna May Wong, Wallace Beery, and George Arlis.

The photographs pre-date to the beginning of the Hays Code era of censorship (circa 1934) and were used as reference for Saunders' sketches in pulp fiction and westerns, with some examples of mirror image sketching.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Saunders' pencil drawing on the verso of a photograph of Jimmy Stewart.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an image of Jack Johnson (1878-1946), the first Black world heavyweight boxing champion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to crime and addiction.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes studio photographs of white actors playing Indigenous characters, a few images featuring Indigenous men as film extras.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographs featuring horses, with pencil drawings by Saunders on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Allen Anderson was a lifelong friend.

did.unittitle: PulpCon group photograph of Saunders, Henry Steeger, Walter Gibson, and others
Color photographs of Saunders studio props

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of letters to Norman Saunders from fans, friends, colleagues, and gallery owners, and a small amount of family correspondence, as well as condolence notes to Ellene Saunders after his death and correspondence about the posthumous sale of his works. Also included are a few photographs and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with empty envelopes filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence is also located in Series I. Visual Material.

did.unittitle: Bonn, Thomas (State University of New York, College at Cortland)
Daniel, Price (governor of Texas, letter of introduction for Saunders museum research trip)
Saunders, Ellene and Norman cards to David Saunders
Saunders, Norman, watercolor and birthday letter to Elva Saunders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of ephemera, printed material, notes and writings, a World War II scrapbook, and other papers by or relating to Norman Saunders. Included is a chronological card catalog of Saunders's published works from 1928 to the early 2010s, and research files and proofs for David Saunders's book Norman Saunders .

did.unittitle: "100 Years of Norman Saunders" by David Saunders
Address book and notecard with addresses

China Then 1943 Now 1994 by B. A. Krawczyk

Letters & copies of publications w/ covers by N.S. / Random poor quality images of N.S. work

Norman Saunders by David Saunders, color proofs of two-page spreads and cover
Norman Saunders by David Saunders, original printer's master proof
Norman Saunders and Ellene Politis marriage certificate
"Norman Saunders' Outlaws of the West: Illustrations for the Pulp Westerns," University of Nebraska-Lincoln exhibition
"Orange Pulp" exhibition poster - University of Syracuse Art Galleries
Popular Mechanics with article on Burma Road
PulpCon #18 Remembers Norm Saunders (badge with illustration of Saunders by Frank Hamilton)
"Scientific Fiction: Imaginary Voyages," Bronx Museum of the Arts exhibition
United States Army "Special Service" booklets on Calcutta and Mumbai
"Who Painted the Wacky Packs?" by David Saunders
Works by Norman Saunders, 1928-1938 (chronological card catalog)
Works by Norman Saunders, 1939-2012 (chronological card catalog)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings on the 1381st Engineer Petroleum Distribution Company's work in India and China, various Army memoranda, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials collected or used by Norman Saunders during his Army service in China in World War II, including an opium pipe, chopsticks, and a sewing kit; Colt pistols and a large ring of keys used as props for his illustrations; general artist tools; and a Polaroid camera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains compact discs with images used in Norman Saunders , a phonograph record for which Saunders contributed album cover art, and a videocassette of an interview of Saunders conducted by his daughter Blaine in their New York home.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
